title: Fix BUG-002: Delegate prereq installation to ansible
notes: TUI no longer acts as package manager. setup.sh bootstraps uv+ansible, ansible/prereqs.yml installs everything else, prerequisites screen just runs the playbook.

title: Add failed state to _TS dataclass and refactor status model
Replace widget teardown with in-place updates
Add error recovery with per-tool retry
Make log panel always visible and delimit per-tool output
Update setup.sh to bootstrap ansible via uv
closed_reason: Added ansible-playbook check + uv tool install ansible to setup.sh

title: Create ansible/prereqs.yml local playbook
closed_reason: Created playbook installing wireguard-tools, sops, age, terraform, porthole with Linux/macOS support and verification tasks

title: Rewrite prerequisites.py as ansible playbook runner
closed_reason: Screen reduced to ~100 lines: runs ansible-playbook in background thread, streams output, shows pass/fail with retry

title: Simplify platform.py — remove install infrastructure
closed_reason: Removed INSTALL_COMMANDS, NEEDS_SUDO, MANUAL_INSTALL_HINTS, get_install_command(), get_manual_hint(). Kept detect_os(), is_installed(), TOOL_DESCRIPTIONS, get_tool_description()

title: Update tests for new prerequisites screen
closed_reason: Rewrote test fixtures to mock subprocess.Popen for ansible. 3 new tests: disabled on mount, enabled after success, retry after failure. All 24 TUI tests pass.

title: Fix BUG-003: Secrets screen porthole init fails — no interactive stdin
notes: All three async workers converted to thread workers. _init_state uses call_from_thread suspend pattern for interactive porthole init. _generate_age_key uses subprocess.run. _write_sops_config uses synchronous file I/O. Added _show_log and _log_markup helpers.

title: Convert _init_state from async worker to thread worker with suspend
description: Change @work(exclusive=True) async def _init_state to @work(thread=True, exclusive=True) def _init_state. Use self.app.call_from_thread to invoke suspend() on the main thread, run porthole init with stdin/stdout/stderr connected to terminal, then resume TUI. Check STATE_PATH after.
closed_reason: Converted to @work(thread=True, exclusive=True) with call_from_thread(_do_init) where _do_init uses with self.app.suspend(): subprocess.run()

title: Convert _generate_age_key and _write_sops_config to thread workers
description: For consistency, convert the other two async workers in secrets.py to thread-based workers using subprocess.run instead of asyncio.create_subprocess_exec. This aligns with the pattern established in prerequisites.py.
closed_reason: Both converted: _generate_age_key uses subprocess.run with capture_output, _write_sops_config uses synchronous file I/O. All UI updates via call_from_thread.
